relyingParties.name: PASS2RENT - Car rental Verifier
relyingParties.description:
PASS2RENT Verifier is an in-house OpenID4VP relying party built and operated by Odifis, a Lithuanian SME, for contactless car rental. One unchanged verifier carries the whole rental journey across two EUDI wallets: a privacy-preserving age check at booking (France Identité PID), driver identification at the vehicle (a test mDL from the EU reference wallet), and a fresh, holder- and device-bound mDL presentation at key handover that releases an ephemeral, replay-protected unlock token.

Privacy is enforced in code, not policy. The rental platform receives a single predicate — age_over_18, with 21/25 thresholds for young-driver pricing — attested directly by the wallet when it serves it (as France Identité does), flagged as derived otherwise. Either way, any birth date disclosed stays at the verification point: read for milliseconds, then stripped before anything is stored or forwarded. No portrait can be stored (no field exists for one); the audit trail is hash-chained and contains zero personal data; claims are encrypted at rest; retention is fail-closed.

Trust is never overstated: the playground PID verifies green against the France Identité IACA trust anchor; an unknown issuer yields an amber "issuer not recognised" — never a silent pass. Revocation uses IETF Token Status Lists. Unevaluated checks are labelled, never simulated — including contract signing: advanced electronic signature (AES) in the demo, not yet legal QES.

Open standards, cross-border by construction: OpenID4VP 1.0 with DCQL, encrypted direct_post.jwt responses, x509_san_dns reader authentication; ISO/IEC 18013-5 mso_mdoc and SD-JWT VC formats. A Lithuanian relying party consuming French national credentials — no bilateral agreement, no national API.

Demo: synthetic identities, real cryptography — not production. Multi-tenant, built so further rental operators can onboard.
